summary: postinstall harvests SCM/cloud identity and exfiltrates to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs on npm install and reads installer-owned identity and cloud-config files — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml,.git/config,.git/logs/HEAD (reflog author emails), ~/.ssh/*.pub (pubkey email comments), /etc/resolv.conf, ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP account), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names) — together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), cwd, and CI environment values. The aggregated fields (scmEmail, githubLogin, sshKeyEmails, recentAuthors, gcpAccount, awsProfiles, hostname, username) are POSTed as JSON via https.request to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a destination unrelated to the declared publisher. In-file comments frame the collection as 'anonymous diagnostics' and claim 'no credentials are transmitted', but the payload contents contradict that framing: personal git email, GitHub identity, SSH pubkey comments, and cloud account identifiers are identity and reconnaissance data useful for targeted follow-on attacks. Installing this package causes automatic, non-consenting exfiltration of developer identity and cloud-account metadata to an attacker-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
